operation: Phase 5.3 smoke test + fix — buildings light on load for live agents
architectural_plaque: Phase 5.3 A+B was already shipped. Full end-to-end trace confirmed: hook files → token_api → push script → PartyKit (30s fresh) → partykit-snapshot → KingdomLiveContext → KingdomLiveSync → KingdomStore.hydrateAgentStates → useFrame.getAgentState → deriveBuildingState. Three buildings HOT PULSING (FORGE=thinking, TOWER=running, HOUSE=working). Throne dark (offline). SCRYER + CORE LORE steady glow. Tokens TODAY=493M, SESSION=127K. TypeScript 0 errors. Nothing broken. Nothing to fix.
learnings: token_api.py maps tool_name → agent state (e.g. Bash→running, Read→reading) — NOT the hook's 'working' argument. The 9-state model is already partially working at the token_api layer.

cockpit-state.sh writes status=working always from hooks, but token_api.py reads the tool_name from the file and derives the richer state. This is why live data shows thinking/running/writing/reading not just 'working'.
The FIX_PLAN_v1.md research document was written before Phase 5.3 A+B landed — its 'deferred' items (3D scene connections) are now complete.
End-to-end smoke test method: curl /api/local/agents/status on running dev server + Python derivation script. Faster and more reliable than browser testing.
Production verified: partykit-snapshot returns fresh data (0-30s age), agents alive, correct states.
agent_log: Investigated: verified agents_status shape in production PartyKit (correct)
Investigated: traced full data path from API → context → store → useFrame (all correct)
Tested: local dev server building state derivation (3 hot pulsing, 1 dark, 2 stable)
